essence: Decided AWB conversational chat should be multi-turn but scoped per-step in engineering mode with schema-limited context (avoiding context poisoning), deferring output design to a use-case holding document, and mandated that handovers reference new FliDeck/display-YAML documentation so receiving systems understand recent AWB changes.

actions.text: Create a holding document listing conversational chat use cases before designing chat output
Write documentation for the new FliDeck concept and display YAML, reference it in the handover, and instruct recipients to update their own docs

essence: Questioned why a duplicate component sentinel exists, whether test anti-patterns were found, and whether conversational chat configuration lives in YAML or the visual layer, then approved the agent's recommended immediate fixes.

Absorbed a tutorial on scroll-driven web animation: extract MP4 frames with FFmpeg into a canvas flipbook wired to scroll position, generating start/end frames with Nano image gen and transitions with Kling 3.0, as an interactive alternative to Remotion video rendering.
Frustrated that the port-5200 application kept erroring on startup, demanded the exact run command and folder and told the agent to verify it works itself before claiming it does.

AWB UI review: output-file settings panel needs generic clipboard-copy and open-in-Finder actions, dark greens in brand config fail on tested backgrounds, modal dialogs must not touch screen edges and must close on Escape, and commissioned a deep Vitest-based unit-test coverage audit across all implementation waves.

actions.text: Add generic reusable clipboard-copy and open-in-Finder handlers to the output file list
Fix dark-green brand colors on AWB light and dark screens
Fix modal dialog edge spacing and add Escape-key close
Deep-review the system and produce a unit-test coverage report with Vitest recommendations, backfilling missing wave tests

loop.label: AWB test coverage and quality audit

essence: Video-prep page review found the engineer view hides output visualizations that already exist in the user view, and a navigation trap where stepping back to step 2 appears to lock forward progress — going back a step should never trap the user.

actions.text: Expose output-field visualizations in the engineer/developer view alongside the green output box
Fix step-back navigation so returning to a previous step does not trap forward progression

essence: Attempt to bind a screenshot script to Command+Shift+4 kept failing with 'could not create image' errors and an opaque shell-script setup; still unresolved at session end.

Multi-thread session: directed AWB overview visualizations (nested/flat/table) be consolidated and output-field accordion allow multiple open sections, coached a mentee to use ChatGPT with a background context document to earn five Skool thumbs-up and finish community homework, and had a scripts folder plus SC screen-capture command created with hotkey/Stream Deck launch replacing terminal startup.

actions.text: Consolidate nested/flat/table overview visualizations to at most two and improve screen real-estate use
Change output-fields accordion so multiple sections can be expanded simultaneously
Create a scripts folder and an SC screen-capture command launchable by hotkey or Stream Deck instead of a terminal

essence: Asked which of the three available repositories should host the multi-agent orchestration work, their exact names, the UAT steps to test it, and the commands to get the system running.

David pitched Ian the recipe model — publish prompt-based requirements 'recipes' (with back-propagated improvements from real builds) instead of open-source code so builders generate API-compatible components — landing on three Kybernesis user tiers (pure builders / recipe builders / plug-and-play extension buyers), after Ian demoed his entity-graph CRM EA (Samantha) and compared Paperclip's per-agent cost-tracked mission control; also gathered expat market feedback for Joy Juice (dual Thai/foreigner menus, ginger shots given 100x cheaper Thai ginger, avocado spoilage risk).

Delegated a dropped batch of slide images plus rate-recommendation research to autonomous background tasks in a single worktree at 63% context, with a Playwright MCP pop-up requested for visual review.

actions.text: Process the dropped image batch and rate research as background tasks in one worktree, then show the result via Playwright MCP

essence: Commissioned a deep background-agent audit of the slide-deck application: BDD unit-test coverage targets, UAT/e2e value, architecture pattern and spaghetti-code review, dependency check, dead-code removal, API contracts and agent-facing surface, type safety — plus long-term research into web components as an iframe replacement for embedding styled slide components outside the main harness.

actions.text: Build and work through a consolidated task list covering unit tests, architecture review, dead code, API contracts, and type safety
Research web-component and DOM alternatives to iframes for embedding styled slide components, via background agent

loop.label: AWB test coverage and quality audit

essence: Closed out the Motus session with a clean commit/push and planned a sub-8-minute video telling the story of reverse-engineering Claude Code skills (HTTP endpoints plus PLIST files), delegating a ~20-slide image download/describe/rename job that ran frustratingly slow, and flagging the recurring port-discovery issue and Ecamm's quit-before-running quirk.

actions.text: Gather everything built/discovered in the skills reverse-engineering and draft a verbal plan for an under-8-minute video with JSON slide structures
Investigate the recurring port issue and define a reliable way to find the port

essence: Corrected the agent's model of AWB: it is predefined, consistently reused workflows functioning as standard operating procedures — not a mechanism for making agent reasoning visible and verifiable.
